evidence_spans.span: A statistical model is a family of probability distributions indexed by an unknown parameter \(\theta\). The likelihood is defined from this statistical model by treating the observations as fixed and comparing possible parameter values.
Maximum likelihood estimation uses the log-likelihood as its objective and selects a parameter value that maximizes it

results.rationale: Maximum likelihood estimation requires a statistical model because the likelihood is defined from the statistical model, and MLE uses the likelihood (or log-likelihood) as its objective.
